rag_rules: Place input range assumptions in component type annexes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Use assign statements in implementation scopes only for output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Generate syntactically valid AGREE annex blocks with proper scope placement for assumptions and guarantees.
generic_rules: Refer to component ports and features using names present in the AADL model structure.
Ensure type consistency between AGREE expressions and AADL data types.
Avoid encoding AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly represented in the model.
Use visible identifiers from the AADL model to construct valid AGREE expressions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference valid component features or declared variables.
Boolean expressions in AGREE must match the actual data types of the referenced features.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes.
Do not assume that AADL property names are automatically available as AGREE variables.
Do not generate range assumptions for Boolean or opaque data types without numeric representation.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing parameters
Model fusion and owner/scope preservation rules
